summary: A very brief post where Shapiro shares a humorous complaint letter written to Continental Airlines about seat 29E. He acknowledges it's completely off-topic from his blog's usual woodworking focus but feels compelled to share it because it's so entertaining. The post is essentially a link-share with minimal commentary, reflecting the casual, personal blogging style common in 2005.
key_points: Shapiro shares a viral complaint letter about Continental Airlines seat 29E
He acknowledges the post is entirely off-topic from his blog's woodworking focus
The post reflects early blogging culture where personal blogs freely mixed topics
money_quotes: Sometimes something comes along that's just so odd, I have to share it with everyone, even though there isn't even the faintest connection between woodworking and the subject at hand.
key_insight: A lighthearted off-topic post sharing a humorous airline complaint letter, notable mainly as an artifact of early blogging culture.
